Row 1 (Leander): "transferred to royal new zealand navy as hmnzs leander 1941-1945\nbroken up at blyth 1950"  
Row 2 (Orion): "broken up at dalmuir, 1949"  
Row 3 (Neptune): "sunk in minefield off tripoli, 19 december 1941"  
Row 4 (Ajax): "broken up at newport, 1949"  
Row 5 (Achilles): "transferred to royal new zealand navy as hmnzs achilles 1941-1946\nsold to indian navy as hims delhi 1948"  
Row 6 (Amphion): "sold to royal australian navy as hmas perth, 1939\nsunk in torpedo attack, 1 march 1942"  
Row 7 (Apollo): "sold to royal australian navy as hmas hobart, 1938\nbroken up at osaka, 1962"  
Row 8 (Sydney): "sunk in surface action, 19 november 1941"  

Leander and Achilles both have similarly lengthy 'fate' descriptions with two events and dates. However, Leander has slightly more content (more words/details) than Achilles. Thus, the Prediction Answer 'leander' is correct as it is the entry with the most content.
